title: llame instance configuration
description: Operator-owned instance configuration for llame (config-as-code, openspec/changes/instance-config). Optional; a setting absent from this file falls back to its legacy environment variable, then a documented built-in default. String values may contain {env:NAME}, {env:NAME:-default}, and {path:LOCATION} interpolation tokens, resolved once at boot. This document is both the editor-facing schema (referenced via the top-level $schema key) and the boot-time validator — the same artifact, so they cannot drift.

interpolationToken.description: A whole-value interpolation token: {env:NAME}, {env:NAME:-default}, or {path:LOCATION}. Only legal on non-string settings as the ENTIRE value (no surrounding text) — string settings may embed tokens anywhere instead.

numberOrToken.description: A positive integer, or a whole-value interpolation token that resolves to one.

nullableNumberOrToken.description: A positive integer, null, or a whole-value interpolation token that resolves to one of those.

nullableString.description: A plain string, or null to explicitly unset (overriding any legacy environment fallback). May embed {env:...}/{path:...} tokens anywhere in the string.

$schema.description: Reference to this published schema, for editor autocomplete/hover. Ignored by the loader — the sole exemption from the closed schema below.

defaults.description: Instance-level model pointers. Deliberately not under a `models` key, which is reserved for the future model-catalog list (providers-and-models-as-code).

modelId.description: Default model id used for new chats when the user hasn't chosen one. Falls back to DEFAULT_MODEL_ID, then unset (model selection fails as a server configuration error at request time).

titleGenerationModelId.description: Model id used to generate chat titles. Falls back to TITLE_GENERATION_MODEL_ID, then unset (title generation is skipped).

runs.description: Durable run execution timers and limits (#48/#50). No compaction.* or context-window setting exists here — compaction is model-driven, never an instance knob.

maxOutputTokens.description: Reserved: cap on generated output tokens per model call. Not yet enforced — no run currently reads this value; it is consumed by the run-budget feature (#91). File-only (no legacy env var). Setting it today has no runtime effect.

heartbeatSeconds.description: How often, in seconds, the executing worker stamps a run's heartbeat. Falls back to RUN_HEARTBEAT_SECONDS, then 15.

heartbeatStaleSeconds.description: A run whose last heartbeat is older than this, in seconds, is considered stale and expirable by the deadman job. Falls back to RUN_HEARTBEAT_STALE_SECONDS, then 60.

timeoutSeconds.description: Delay, in seconds, before a run's first liveness check after enqueue. Falls back to RUN_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, then 300.

trustProxy.description: Express trust-proxy setting: a non-negative integer hop count, 'true'/'false', or a subnet spec. Falls back to TRUST_PROXY, then unset (no proxy trusted — client IPs are taken at face value).

tools.description: Tool-calling loop availability (openspec/changes/tool-calling-loop). The operator allowlist is the entire availability story this slice — no policy engine exists yet (D3).

allowed.description: Registered tool ids admitted for advertisement/execution. Default: empty array (no tools advertised or executable — fail closed). An id not registered in the code-owned tool registry fails boot naming the path and id.

maxStepsPerRun.description: Hard cap on tool-requesting steps per run before the loop stops offering tools and drives the model to answer from accumulated context. Falls back to the built-in default, 8.

callTimeoutSeconds.description: Global timeout, in seconds, for a single tool call (a tool may declare a per-tool override at registration — a code-level property, not a config key). Falls back to the built-in default, 15.
